directive_title: Decider verify-push gap + queue archive-on-pop gap + analyst-baby dispatcher filter discipline + 4 zombie blocked-on-curmudgeon ISSs

backlog_trend.note: Backlog down 44% WoW; net velocity +14 (closed 22, new 8). PROP-055/056 landings this morning compressed the metric from yesterday's MAJOR-tier 34 to today's 20. Both PROPs achieved their stated metric_correctness goal.

step_1_decider_verify_push_gap.case: decider-2026-05-25T09-20 popped WIN-067 qid=402, self-applied 2 minor patches (code_analysis.monitoring tag + Mascon redundancy), queue depth returned to 0. No follow-up qid=403 was pushed for WIN-067 c14 verification.
step_1_decider_verify_push_gap.diagnosis: MISSING-BY-OMISSION (not missing-by-condition). All three prompt locations REFERENCE the rule ('when Step 5 self-apply pushes the patched target for re-review, set class:verification per PROP-025') but no concrete procedure exists in monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md for actually constructing the verify-class queue push. The 'Self-Apply procedure' (Step 6b, lines 55+) walks through clone setup, patch application, close-with-pending-verification, and cleanup — but stops before any queue push. The LLM is expected to remember to do the push from its own knowledge of decider.md L378, with the shape from decider.md L840+ ('How to push items onto the queue'). The rule has no procedural anchor and is consequently easy to miss.

review_meta_for_WIN_067_c13.fits_PROP_038_phase_1_gate: yes (≤2 minor holes, both fixed by self-apply, current_verdict_holds=true) — verify-class push would be the right action per the eligibility criteria, so this is a pure missed-push, not a conditional skip

proposed_fix.scope: Add an explicit 'Step 7: Push verify-class re-review onto priority queue' subsection to decider-patches-and-selfapply.md, AFTER the cleanup section. Insert a code template that constructs the push using the patched target's metadata. Mirror the existing 'How to push items onto the queue' template at decider.md L840+.
proposed_fix.blast_radius: Minimal — adds explicit code that the LLM was expected to construct from memory. Removes a 'phantom-step' pattern.
proposed_fix.operator_decision: Tinker recommends operator-side apply (one-line prompt edit) rather than a full PROP since the procedural code already exists in decider.md L840+ and the fix is to lift+paste with the verify-class default. Operator may choose to author a small PROP-059 if a formal review is preferred.

step_1_decider_verify_push_gap.operator_one_line_action: Insert a 'Step 7: Verify-class re-review push' subsection in monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md immediately before the '## Cleanup (mandatory, run last)' section, using the code shape above. After insertion, the verify-push becomes a documented procedure step rather than an LLM-remembered abstraction.

step_2_queue_archive_on_pop_gap.operator_observed_state: FUSE workspace ${WORKSPACE}/monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue-archive.jsonl ended at qid=395 popped 2026-05-21T06:05:00Z. qids 396-402 missing.
step_2_queue_archive_on_pop_gap.tinker_verification: git canonical (origin/main HEAD = 442f5a7) priority-queue-archive.jsonl HAS qids 396-402, all present, all with strict_queue_id pop_reason and claimed_review_file fields. FUSE was stale by 7 entries (276 vs 269 lines).
step_2_queue_archive_on_pop_gap.diagnosis: NOT a decider pop-procedure gap. The pop→archive append in monitor/prompts/decider.md L766 (`fs.appendFileSync(historyArchivePath, lines)`) is working correctly — qids 396-402 are in git canonical. The gap is FUSE staleness: priority-queue-archive.jsonl is classified `git`-owned (build.js OWNERSHIP L95-area, see workspace-sync.md L413 `smart_copy ${WORKSPACE}/monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue-archive.jsonl monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue-archive.jsonl`). workspace-sync's smart_copy is FUSE→git direction-aware with an mtime guard — when git is newer than FUSE (decider pushed direct), the guard skips the copy. The git→FUSE direction is supposed to be handled by `build.js publish`, but the decider doesn't run build.js publish after committing the queue archive — it just commits and pushes. So FUSE accumulates lag every time decider pops without a subsequent build.js publish.
step_2_queue_archive_on_pop_gap.fuse_backfill_action_taken: Tinker appended the 7 missing entries (qids 396-402) from git canonical to FUSE this run. md5 hashes now match: 7cf349eca4f4c56ec8f6b79d455e8dbf for both. Operator-side FUSE view of priority-queue-archive.jsonl now reflects truth.
step_2_queue_archive_on_pop_gap.structural_fix_consideration: Two options: (a) Add priority-queue-archive.jsonl to a git→FUSE one-shot copy in workspace-sync's startup (it would lift the latest git copy and replace FUSE when git is newer); (b) Have decider call a one-line 'cp clone/.../priority-queue-archive.jsonl WORKSPACE/.../' after the appendFileSync. Option (a) is closer to the workspace-sync philosophy ('round-trip safety'); option (b) is closer to the decider's universal-pusher pattern ('write to both sides'). Tinker recommends (b) — same pattern decider already uses for data/wins.json etc. — and notes this should be a small PROP if pursued.
step_2_queue_archive_on_pop_gap.operator_one_line_action: If you want this to self-heal going forward without a PROP, the simplest patch is: in monitor/prompts/decider.md, immediately after the line `fs.appendFileSync(historyArchivePath, lines);` (~L766), add `try{ fs.appendFileSync('${WORKSPACE}/monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue-archive.jsonl', lines); }catch(_){}` so the decider writes to both git and FUSE simultaneously. This mirrors the dual-write pattern already used for human-notes.json (see CLAUDE.md 'Human Notes Rule').
step_2_queue_archive_on_pop_gap.tinker_followup: FUSE backfilled this run (md5 match). Will re-verify on next run.

step_3_analyst_baby_dispatcher.audit_scope: Both analyst-baby (monitor/prompts/analyst-baby.md L94-103) and main analyst (monitor/prompts/analyst.md L143-154) dispatcher filters.

filter_trace_baby.EXP_425_evaluation: status='pending' ✓, !blocked_on ✓, claimed_by !== 'analyst' ✓ (undefined). babyOwns(): review_class undefined (skip 'deep-attack/holistic' check), review_class undefined (skip 'verification' check), review_class null+source='decider-bau-triage' NOT in babyOwnedSources {'decider-bau-route','decider-m1-route','decider-m3-carry-over'} → babyOwns returns false. ITEM CORRECTLY EXCLUDED.
filter_trace_baby.conclusion_baby: Both items are CORRECTLY excluded by the existing analyst-baby filter. No bug in baby's dispatcher for these specific cases.

filter_trace_main_analyst.EXP_425_evaluation: status='pending' ✓, !blocked_on ✓, claimed_by !== 'analyst-baby' ✓ (undefined), !babyOwns(EXP-425) → !false → true ✓. ITEM MATCHES — gets surfaced to Opus analyst every run.
filter_trace_main_analyst.conclusion_main: **BUG CONFIRMED in main analyst dispatcher for EXP-425.** The filter has NO assigned_to guard. EXP-425 with assigned_to='tinker' should not be surfaced to the analyst (Opus) but is. This is the cause of the 3-consecutive-run Opus no-op on EXP-425.

fix_proposal.summary: Add `(i.assigned_to == null || i.assigned_to === 'analyst' || i.assigned_to === 'analyst-baby')` guard to BOTH dispatcher filters (defense-in-depth — baby's filter doesn't bite today but would if a tinker-assigned item ever had review_class='verification', and the guard makes intent explicit).

main_analyst_patch.rationale: One-line addition. Excludes items where decider explicitly assigned to a non-analyst owner (e.g. 'tinker'). Conservative: items with no assigned_to (typical case) still pass; explicit analyst/analyst-baby assignments still pass.

baby_patch_optional_defense_in_depth.rationale: Defensive: prevents future tinker/social/integrity-assigned items from being picked up by baby if review_class somehow matched.

step_3_analyst_baby_dispatcher.secondary_observation: EXP-431 currently has the correct exclusion (status='consolidated-into-EXP-432'). However, the dispatcher logic could be more robust by also checking for status prefix 'consolidated-into-' explicitly, since the 'pending' check is the ONLY thing keeping consolidated items out. If a future close site accidentally writes status='pending' on a consolidated item, both dispatchers would surface it. Tinker recommends a follow-up PROP that adds an explicit `!/^consolidated-into-/.test(item.status)` defensive guard, but this is not urgent — current behavior is correct.

step_4_zombie_iss_sweep_PROP_authored.title: decider end-of-run sweep: close status='blocked-on-curmudgeon' issues whose underlying EXP is integrated OR whose blocker ISS is closed; one-time burndown of 4 zombie entries
step_4_zombie_iss_sweep_PROP_authored.design_pattern: Mirrors PROP-056 Step A0 structure. New Step A0b sweep walks open-issues.json for status='blocked-on-curmudgeon', resolves each via (a) extractExpId() + expIntegrated map lookup (closes via 'exp-integrated-burndown'), OR (b) extractBlockerIss() + closedSet lookup (closes via 'blocker-iss-closed-burndown'). Idempotent, ledger-deduped, fail-loud self-test.
burndown_targets: ISS-1599 (blocker ISS-1605 closed 2026-04-27 — operator caveat re: WCAG contrast)
step_4_zombie_iss_sweep_PROP_authored.operator_caveats_called_out: ISS-1599 has substantive WCAG-AA contrast concern. Burndown script defaults to INCLUDE but PROP recommends operator manually verify dark-mode contrast on live site before close, or re-open as fresh ISS if contrast still fails.
step_4_zombie_iss_sweep_PROP_authored.follow_up_recommendation: Consider authoring a small follow-up PROP that adds `blocker_iss_id: 'ISS-NNNN'` as a structured field whenever decider creates a blocked-on-curmudgeon ISS pointing at another ISS. Would replace the fragile free-text 'blocked on ISS-NNNN' regex with structured lookup and enable forward-walk at ISS-closure time.

step_5_verify_push_recurrence_audit.method: Scanned monitor/decisions/daily-report-decider-*.json for patches_applied non-empty, extracted target_ids (handling 3 different shape conventions: {win, pred, target_id, file+pred, target:'data/x.json:Y.field'}). For each target, scanned priority-queue-archive.jsonl for any pop with target_id match AND popped_at within +1h..+7d of the patch-run timestamp.
step_5_verify_push_recurrence_audit.result: 3 decider runs with patches in window. 2 of 3 have NO follow-up verify-class pop within 7d — pattern is recurring.

table.status: PARTIAL (2/3) — WIN edits got verify-pushed, PRED-106 did not
MISS (0/1) — the trigger case for this directive

step_5_verify_push_recurrence_audit.summary: 3 runs with patches; 0 full-hit runs, 1 partial-hit, 2 full misses. PRED-target patches in particular have a 0/2 hit rate (PRED-106 on 5/13, PRED-073 on 5/24); WIN-target patches have a 3/4 hit rate (WIN-028 and WIN-017 5/13 hit; WIN-067 5/25 miss). The 'WIN gets verify-pushed sometimes; PRED never' pattern is suggestive but n is too small to draw causal conclusions. The aggregate signal — 2 of last 3 runs missing — confirms the discipline gap is real and recurring, not a one-off.
step_5_verify_push_recurrence_audit.recommendation: Apply the Step 1 procedural fix (insert explicit 'Step 7: Verify-class re-review push' subsection in decider-patches-and-selfapply.md). Re-audit after 1 week of decider runs to confirm the procedural anchor closes the gap.

summary_one_paragraph: Operator directive DIRECTIVE-20260525-002 executed. Step 1 (decider verify-push gap): diagnosed as missing-by-omission — three prompts reference the rule but no procedural code anchors it; recommended one-line operator fix to insert 'Step 7: Verify-class re-review push' subsection in decider-patches-and-selfapply.md. Step 2 (queue archive gap): NOT a decider bug — git canonical has all 7 missing qids; FUSE was 7 entries stale (git-owned file, workspace-sync only handles FUSE→git); tinker backfilled FUSE this run; recommended one-line dual-write fix in decider.md so FUSE stays current going forward. Step 3 (analyst-baby dispatcher): confirmed bug in MAIN ANALYST dispatcher (not baby) — no assigned_to guard, so EXP-425 (assigned_to:'tinker') keeps surfacing to Opus; one-line filter addition fixes. Step 4: PROP-058 authored — Step A0b sweep mirroring PROP-056 Step A0, plus one-time burndown of 4 zombies (ISS-1351, ISS-1552, ISS-1553, ISS-1599); operator caveat on ISS-1599 (substantive WCAG contrast concern needs separate verification). Step 5 (recurrence audit): 14d window — 3 decider runs with patches, 0 full-hit, 1 partial-hit, 2 full misses. Verify-push discipline gap is real and recurring; Step 1 fix would close it.
